tasks.title: Redact secrets and filesystem paths from job APIs (and MCP job_status)
evidence: internal/model/job.go defines Job with Params, ResultPath, and Error fields that may contain secrets and local filesystem paths.
internal/api/jobs.go returns model.Job values directly in GET /v1/jobs and GET /v1/jobs/{id}, exposing Params and ResultPath to clients.
internal/mcp/server.go tool job_status returns s.store.Get(ctx, id), which includes Job.ResultPath and Job.Params as stored.
plan: Define API-safe job response types (e.g., JobSummary/JobDetail) that omit ResultPath and redact sensitive Params fields (auth, login, headers, cookies, tokens).
Update API handlers in internal/api/jobs.go (and any other endpoints returning model.Job) to return the safe types.
Update MCP job_status (and any other MCP responses returning model.Job) to use the safe type or a redacted view.
Update api/openapi.yaml schemas to match the new response shapes and regenerate TS client (make generate).
Update web UI to consume the new job response types.
Add/extend tests ensuring secrets and paths are not returned (API + MCP).
Run make test-ci and make ci.

tasks.title: Lock down DATA_DIR permissions and write job artifacts with 0600
evidence: internal/store/store.go Open() creates DATA_DIR with os.MkdirAll(dataDir, 0o755), leaving job DB directory world-readable/executable by default.
internal/auth/vault.go SaveVault() uses os.MkdirAll(dataDirOrDefault(dataDir), 0o755) even though auth vault files are secret-bearing.
internal/jobs/manager.go run() uses os.MkdirAll(resultDir, 0o755) and os.Create(job.ResultPath), which creates result files with process umask defaults (not guaranteed 0600).
plan: Define a single helper for ensuring DATA_DIR exists with secure permissions (prefer 0700) and reuse it across store/auth/scheduler/jobs.
Write job result files and other secret-bearing artifacts using os.OpenFile with explicit 0600 perms (and directories 0700).
Decide and document whether to chmod existing directories/files on startup (migration) vs only apply to new writes.
Add tests that assert created directories and files have expected mode bits (skip/adjust on Windows if needed).
Run make test and make ci.

tasks.title: Redact querystrings/userinfo from URLs in logs and log only safe error messages
evidence: internal/jobs/manager.go logs full URLs via slog.Info("processing scrape job", "url", url) and slog.Error("scrape job failed", "url", url, ...).
internal/research/research.go logs full URLs via slog.Debug("research crawling target", "url", target, ...) and slog.Error("research crawl failed", "url", target, ...).
internal/crawl/crawl.go logs per-page URLs via slog.Debug("enqueuing crawl task", "url", url, ...) which may include tokens in query params.
plan: Introduce a shared URL sanitization helper for logs (strip query, fragment, and userinfo; optionally truncate long paths).
Replace slog fields that log raw URL strings with sanitized output across jobs/crawl/research (and any other occurrences).
Ensure error logs use apperrors.SafeMessage(err) (or equivalent) instead of raw err when the error may contain secrets or filesystem paths.
Add tests for URL sanitization edge cases (userinfo, query tokens, fragments, invalid URLs) and a small regression test covering at least one log site.
Run make test and make ci.

tasks.title: Make MCP tools/list JSON schema output deterministic (stable key ordering)
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go schema() builds the JSON schema by ranging over Go maps (required/optional), which produces nondeterministic property and required key ordering.
MCP tools/list output is part of the client-facing contract; nondeterministic ordering can cause flaky snapshots and caching issues for consumers.
plan: Refactor schema() to iterate keys in sorted order for both properties and required arrays.
Add a regression test that calls tools/list multiple times and asserts identical JSON output (or at least identical schema key ordering).
Run make test and make ci.

tasks.title: Remove fetch-mocking from web tests and replace with non-mocked coverage
evidence: web/src/App.test.tsx uses vi.stubGlobal("fetch", ...) and vi.fn().mockResolvedValueOnce(...) to simulate API responses.
Repository guidelines require tests in make ci to avoid mocks; current web tests violate that constraint.
plan: Decide target strategy: (A) convert to integration tests that run against a real local API server, or (B) extract pure parsing/formatting helpers and test them directly without mocking fetch.
Refactor App.tsx (or web/src/lib) so the logic under test is accessible without stubbing globals.
Replace App.test.tsx with tests that cover success/failure modes using the chosen non-mock approach.
Ensure web test suite remains fast and deterministic under make test-ci.
Run make test-ci and make ci.

tasks.title: Add open-source release essentials (LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, CODE_OF_CONDUCT, SECURITY)
evidence: Repository root has no LICENSE/COPYING file (no matches for LICENSE*).
Repository root has no CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, or SECURITY.md files.
plan: Choose an explicit license (MIT/Apache-2.0/etc) and add LICENSE file.
Add CONTRIBUTING.md with setup, make targets, coding standards, and how to report bugs/security issues.
Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md (e.g., Contributor Covenant) and reference it from README.md.
Add SECURITY.md describing how to report vulnerabilities and expected response process.
Run make ci to ensure docs additions don’t break tooling.

tasks.title: Split `internal/jobs/manager.go` (733 LOC) and define correct shutdown semantics
evidence: internal/jobs/manager.go is 733 lines, exceeding the 700 LOC threshold that requires explicit justification and is a maintainability risk.
internal/jobs/manager.go Start() drains and runs queued jobs after ctx.Done(), but uses the canceled context, which risks spurious failures or surprising behavior during shutdown.
plan: Split manager.go into cohesive files (e.g., manager.go, worker.go, job_create.go, job_run.go) while keeping package boundaries stable.
Define and document shutdown behavior: whether to stop processing immediately, drain without running, or drain with a new bounded context.
Adjust worker loop implementation to match the decided semantics and add tests for shutdown + queued jobs.
Run make test and make ci.

tasks.title: Prepare Go module path for public consumption (set module to final GitHub import path)
evidence: go.mod declares `module spartan-scraper`, which is not a resolvable public import path for `go install` once the repo is public.
plan: Decide the final public repo path (e.g., github.com/<org>/spartan-scraper).
Update go.mod module directive to the final path and update all internal imports accordingly.
Verify `go build` and `go test ./...` work in a clean checkout and that `make build` still installs the binary.
Update README.md with a `go install ...@latest` installation option (if desired).
Run make ci.

tasks.title: Make OpenAPI post-processing script portable (remove perl/rg hard deps or add checks)
evidence: scripts/strip_openapi_todos.sh shells out to `rg` and `perl` to modify generated TS files.
Makefile `generate` target invokes scripts/strip_openapi_todos.sh, so missing rg/perl breaks generation for new contributors.
plan: Decide whether to (A) replace the script with a Node-based implementation (using repo’s existing Node toolchain) or (B) implement robust fallbacks to grep/sed and avoid perl.
Add explicit preflight checks with actionable error messages (which dependency is missing and how to install).
Update docs/usage.md (or README) to list required tooling for `make generate`.
Run make generate and make ci.

tasks.title: Sync root `.env` with `.env.example` (MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES and other config drift)
evidence: internal/config/config.go loads MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES into Config.MaxResponseBytes with a default (10MB).
Root .env.example includes MAX_RESPONSE_BYTES, but root .env does not, creating config drift for local runs.
plan: Decide canonical config list and ensure root .env and .env.example contain the same keys (with safe defaults).
Update docs/usage.md configuration section to match the actual config keys consumed by internal/config/config.go.
Add a small test or lint check (optional) that ensures .env.example and .env stay aligned (keys match).

tasks.title: Add `make ci-slow` (stress test) and align docs with actual CI gate
evidence: Makefile defines `ci: install generate format type-check lint build test-ci` and `test-ci` excludes `internal/e2e` and only runs `cd web && pnpm run test`.
docs/usage.md section "CI Coverage" claims `make ci` covers MCP, scheduler, web preview smoke test, and external auth targets, which is not reflected in Makefile targets.
scripts/stress_test.sh exists and performs real-target checks across CLI + API + MCP + scheduler + web build, but it is not wired into Make targets.
plan: Add a `ci-slow` (or `e2e`) target that runs `scripts/stress_test.sh` (and/or `make test` including internal/e2e) with clear expectations and timeouts.
Update docs/usage.md and README.md to clearly separate `make ci` (fast local gate) from `make ci-slow` (real targets/headless).
Ensure `make ci` remains deterministic and reasonably fast; keep network/headless in the slow target.
Add minimal validation to `scripts/stress_test.sh` so failures are actionable (preflight checks, clearer output).
Run make ci and (optionally) make ci-slow.

tasks.title: Harden `scripts/stress_test.sh` prerequisites and remove implicit `python` dependency
evidence: scripts/stress_test.sh shells out to `python -c` for JSON parsing (job status/id extraction), which may not exist as `python` on many systems (python3 only).
scripts/stress_test.sh also assumes curl, sed, and make/pnpm are available but does not preflight or explain missing deps beyond generic failures.
plan: Add a preflight section that checks required commands and prints actionable install hints (go, pnpm, curl, etc.).
Replace `python -c` usage with a more predictable approach (prefer `jq` if present, or a small Node one-liner using the repo’s Node toolchain).
Ensure `--help` documents prerequisites explicitly and includes at least one end-to-end example.
If `make ci-slow` is added, wire it to use the improved script and keep outputs stable.
Run `scripts/stress_test.sh --help` and (optionally) a short smoke run against example.com.

tasks.title: Docs parity sweep: CLI commands and API endpoints vs actual router
evidence: docs/usage.md "Global entry points" list omits commands that exist in internal/cli/cli.go routing (templates, crawl-states, jobs, health).
docs/usage.md API endpoint list does not include every route registered by internal/api/server.go (e.g., /v1/schedules, /v1/templates, /v1/crawl-states).
plan: List all CLI commands from internal/cli/help.go and internal/cli/cli.go and ensure docs/usage.md reflects them with concise examples.
List all API routes from internal/api/server.go and ensure docs/usage.md endpoint list matches (or justify omissions).
Verify api/openapi.yaml covers all API routes (and update if gaps exist).
Run make generate to ensure TS client remains in sync after any OpenAPI edits.

tasks.title: Fix README web-dev proxy docs (use web/.env VITE_API_BASE_URL, not root PORT)
evidence: README.md states the Web UI dev server proxies to `PORT` from root `.env`.
web/vite.config.ts uses VITE_API_BASE_URL (or defaults to http://localhost:8741) and does not read the root `.env`.
plan: Update README.md Web UI section to point users at `web/.env` / `web/.env.example` and VITE_API_BASE_URL for proxy target.
Clarify how to run backend on a non-default port and how to configure the web proxy accordingly.
Run make ci to ensure docs-only change doesn’t break formatting/linting.

tasks.title: Add `spartan version` and unify version across CLI, OpenAPI, and MCP
evidence: internal/mcp/server.go responds to initialize with a hard-coded version string "0.1.0".
api/openapi.yaml info.version is "0.1.0".
CLI help (internal/cli/help.go) does not expose a version command or build metadata.
plan: Introduce a single version source (e.g., internal/buildinfo with Version/Commit/Date variables set via ldflags).
Add a `spartan version` subcommand and include version in `spartan --help` output (or top banner).
Plumb version into MCP initialize response and OpenAPI info.version (generate-time or runtime).
Update Makefile build to pass ldflags for version/commit/date.
Add tests that assert version is surfaced in CLI output and MCP initialize response.
Run make ci.

tasks.title: Remove stray empty directory `spartan-scraper/internal/api/` from repo
evidence: Repository contains an empty `spartan-scraper/internal/api/` directory at the root (separate from `internal/api/`), which looks like an accidental artifact.
plan: Confirm whether the directory is tracked in git (and why it exists).
Remove the directory if it is accidental; ensure no tooling depends on it.
If the directory is generated, add/adjust ignore rules so it never shows up in clean checkouts.
Run make ci.
